# Impedance Control with M0609 (Doosan)

> A personal research project by Seunghwan Um and Tae Hyun Bae at Sungkyunkwan University.

## Overview

This project implements impedance control on a Doosan M0609 collaborative robot arm.
Position control alone is vulnerable to contact during teleoperation — when the robot encounters continuous force, impact, or constrained environments (e.g., pivoting), it cannot react compliantly.

Impedance control enables:
- Stable contact maintenance during tasks involving continuous force or impact
- Safe operation in constrained environments such as pivoting tasks
- Compliant response to external disturbances during teleoperation

## Demonstrations

- **Erasing task**: Position control fails upon contact, while impedance control maintains stable contact with the surface.
- **Pivoting task**: Position control struggles in constrained motion, while impedance control enables smooth pivoting despite external constraints.

## Links

- Project page: https://r-ush.github.io/m0609_impedance_control/
- Code: https://github.com/r-ush/m0609_impedance_control
- Author homepage: https://r-ush.github.io
